original: St Hilary begins his reign as Catholic Pope
K'ak Chan Yopaat [Smoke Snake] aged 15 becomes the 11th ruler of the Maya city of Copán, will go on to rule for 49 years
Mongol army lands at Hakata Bay in Japan during their first invasion attempt and are defeated; a typhoon destroys most of their fleet as they withdraw
League of Cologne goes against Danish King Valdemar IV
The final decree of the Diet of Augsburg, "The Recess," is signed byCharles Vand Catholic princes, reaffirming Catholic rites and principles after the departure of Protestant princes
Spanish Mendaña Expedition departs Callao, Peru, led by Alvaro de Mendaña de Neira and his uncle Pedro Sarmiento de Gamboa to explore the Pacific (1st Europeans to discover Solomon Islands)
Dutch ship Nieuw Hoorn explodes in the Sunda Strait, near Sumatra, after a fire on board reaches the gunpowder, killing over 100 men
Rabbi Isaiah ben Avraham Ha-Levi Horowitz returns to the Holy Land settles in Jerusalem
First protestant ministry society in New England
American Revolutionary War: US forces abandon Fort Lee, New Jersey and retreat to Pennsylvania after British and Hessian troops take Fort Washington, New York
Jakobin Club forms in Paris
Jay Treaty, first US extradition treaty, signed with Great Britain
Dutch Rijksmuseum founded in the Hague (moved to Amsterdam 1808)
Warsaw University is established
Storm causes flooding in St Petersburg, killing 10,000
A ship carrying 500 armed Maori from Ngati Tama and Ngati Mutunga arrives on the Chatham Islands; those Moriori (indigenous people of the Chatham Islands) that are not killed are enslaved
Floridsdorf-Deutsch Wagram railway in Austria opens
American poet and abolitionist Julia Ward Howe commits lyrics of "Battle Hymn of the Republic" to paper
The first petroleum shipment (1,329 barrels) from the U.S. to Europe leaves Philadelphia, USA, for London, England on the Elizabeth Watts
Hudson Bay Company signs deed of transfer of Prince Rupert's Land (3.9 million sq. km) to the British Crown, who then cedes it to Canada for £300,000, or $1.5 million (effective 1870)
E.D. Barbour of Boston is awarded the first U.S. patent for the first 'calculator', an adding machine capable of printing totals and subtotals
James Reed and two accomplices rob the Watt Grayson family of $30,000 in the Choctaw Nation
National Association of Trotting Horse Breeders determines what "is" a trotter
A meteorite lands near the village of Großliebenthal, southwest of Odessa, Ukraine
Bulgarians, led by Stefan Stambolov, repulse a larger Serbian invasion force at Slivinitza
Dutch troops occupy and plunder the palace of Tjakra Negara in Lombok
First mushroom appears on a stamp (China 1 & 5 Ap)
American inventor Frederick E. Blaisdell of Philadelphia patents the "self-sharpening" paper-sheathed pencil, commonly known as a china marker
Temperance activist Carrie Nation attempts to address the US Senate
The steamer "Hilda" sinks in the English Channel (100 people drown)
London is selected to host the 1908 Olympics
Ferenc Molnàr's play "Tester" premieres in Budapest
Demonstration for a French Language University in Ghent
The Oklahoma State Senate ousts Governor Walton for anti-Ku Klux Klan measures
British miners' strike ends after 28 weeks
Joe Kershalla scores 71 points in a college football game
Women allowed to vote in Spain (helps right wing)
Comic superheroes Flash (as Jay Garrick) and Hawkman (Carter Hall) first appear in "Flash Comics No. 1" published by DC
Don Lash wins 6th straight AAU cross-country 10K championship
Belgian King Leopold III visitsAdolf Hitler
First major German air raid on Birmingham - about 440 bombers kill 450
Operation Uranus: Soviet offensive begins during the Battle of Stalingrad, 1 million Soviet soldiers encircle the German Sixth Army
Russia launches winter offensive against Germans along Don front
200" mirror arrives at Mt Palomar Observatory, California
US performs nuclear test at Nevada Test Site
North American F-86 Sabre sets world aircraft speed record, 1124 KPH
Scandinavian Airlines opens a commercial route from Canada to Europe
Spain joins UNESCO
US Supreme Court rules (7-2) that baseball is a sport, not a business
KXMB TV channel 12 in Bismarck, North Dakota (CBS/ABC) begins broadcasting
National Review publishes its first issue
Antonin Novotny appointed president of Czechoslovakia
First 2 F-27 Fokker's Friendships delivered on Aer Lingus

1st episode of Jay Ward's cartoon series Rocky and His Friends", featuring Rocket J. ("Rocky") Squirrel and Bullwinkle J. Moose. airs on ABC; voice talents of June Foray, Paul Frees, and William Conrad
Ford Motors cancels its poorly received Edsel model
Mickey Vernon is hired as 1st manager of new Washington team
KOET (now KULC) TV channel 9 in Ogden, UT (PBS) begins broadcasting
SN Behrman's "Lord Pengo" premieres in NYC
The Paul Winter Sextet performs the first jazz concert at the White House, at the invitation of First Lady Jacqueline Kennedy
Todor Zhivkov (51) becomes Prime Minister of Bulgaria
ABC radio begins weekly "Vietnam Update" report
Kellogg's Pop Tarts pastries created
Mad Dog Vachon beats Dick The Bruiser in Omaha, to become NWA champ
The establishment of TVB, the first wireless commercial television station in Hong Kong
Military coup in Mali, president Modibo Keita flees
New York Yankees pitcher Stan Bahnsen wins AL Rookie of the Year
Apollo 12's Charles Conrad and Alan Bean become the 3rd and 4th humans on the Moon
WENY TV channel 36 in Elmira, NY (ABC) begins broadcasting
Golden Gate Park Conservatory becomes a California state historical landmark
US performs nuclear test at Nevada Test Site
Disney World's Fort Wilderness opens
Gershwin Theater (Uris) opens at 1633 Broadway NYC
KFIZ TV channel 34 in Fond du Lac, WI suspends broadcasting
Leader of the Irish Republican Army (IRA) Seán MacStiofáin is arrested in Dublin
Elections in Suriname, premier Sedney's PNP doesn't win a chair
New York stock market takes sharpest drop in 19 years
Jaime Ornelas Camacho takes office as the first President of the Regional Government of Madeira, Portugal
Canuck's Ron Sedlbauer fails on 3rd penalty shot against Islanders
Libya drops diplomatic relations with Egypt
"The Miracle at the Meadowlands", Eagles Herman Edwards returns a fumble for a touchdown with 31 seconds left to give Philadelphia a 19-17 victory over the rival New York Giants
U.S. Steel agrees to pay $6.3 billion for Marathon Oil
Robert Whitney (79) conducts his final concert, leading the Louisville Orchestra at the opening of the Whitney Concert Hall at the Kentucky Center for the Performing Arts in Louisville, Kentucky
Liquid gas tank in Mexico City explodes; 334 die
In the largest civil verdict in US history, Pennzoil wins $10.53 billion judgement against Texaco
Tina Howe's "Coastal Disturbances" premieres in NYC
France performs nuclear test
US beats Trinidad, 1-0 qualifing for 1990 world soccer cup finals it was US' 1st qualification since 1950
Greyhound files reoganization plan so they can be traded publicly
Pop duo Milli Vanilli are stripped of their Grammy Award after it is learned they did not sing on their award-winning "Girl You Know It's True" album
Algerian Muslim fundamentalists uprising, 27 killed
Curacaose vote to remain part of Dutch Antilles
First drawing of UK's National Lottery takes place, administered by the Camelot Group, a franchised private operator
"The Beatles Anthology" documentary series premieres in US on ABC-TV; the 6-hour program airs over three nights that week
Keelin Curnuck, 23, Ms Venus Swimwear 1994, crowned Miss NY USA
Suicide bomber blasts into Egyptian embassy in Islamabad, kills 16
Albert Bellesigns a record five-year, $55 million contract with the White Sox
Case of the Port Arthur massacre comes to trial in Hobart
Lieutenant General Maurice Baril of Canada arrives in Africa to lead a multi-national policing force in Zaire
Space Shuttle STS 80 (Columbia 21), launches into space
The 12.9 km Confederation Bridge, joining Borden-Carleton, Prince Edward Island and Cape Jourimain, New-Brunswick is completed and becomes the longest bridge over ice covered waters in the world
"Eugene Onegin" opens at Martin Beck Theater NYC
"Old Neighborhood" opens at Booth Theater NYC
McCaughey septuplets (all 7 of them!) born to Bobbi and Kenny McCaughey in Des Moines, Iowa. First set of septuplets to survive infancy.
STS 87 (Columbia 24) launches into orbit
China launches its first Shenzhou spacecraft, Shenzhou 1, in an uncrewed flight
Organization for Security and Cooperation in Europe ends a two-day summit in Istanbul by calling for a political settlement in Chechnya and adopts a Charter for European Security
New Zealand suffers its worst mining disaster since 1914 when the first of four explosions occurs at the Pike River Mine; 29 people are killed
Plymouth Crown Court clears two farmers of health and safety offenses related to giant hay bale death of ELO cellist Mike Edwards
23 people are killed by a suicide bombing attack on the Iranian Embassy in Beirut, Lebanon
Bulgarian tennis star Gregor Dimitrov breaks through for a first season-ending ATP World Tour Finals title with a 7–5, 4–6, 6–3 win in London over David Goffin of Belgium

Oumuamua first detected by scientists, 400 meters long and reddish, first known interstellar object in our solar system, thought to be of possible alien origin (since thought to be slice of small planet)
Nissan chairman Carlos Ghosn arrested in Japan for financial misconduct
As many as 106 have died over five days in protests across 21 Iranian cities according to Amnesty International, just 12 deaths reported by the government
EPL club Tottenham sacks high profile manager Mauricio Pochettino after disappointing start to season; replacement is higher profile ex-Chelsea and Manchester United boss Jose Mourinho
More than 1,000 protesters detained by police after 3-day battle at Hong King Polytechnic University
Inquiry find "credible evidence" elite Australian troops unlawfully killed 39 Afghan civilians
Scottish American Douglas Stuart wins the Booker Literary prize for his first novel "Shuggie Bain"
Austria becomes the first country to make the COVID-19 vaccine mandatory (from 1 Feb) as it announces its fourth lockdown
Longest partial lunar eclipse since 1440, lasting 3 hours, 28 minutes and 23 seconds
MLB Cleveland Indians name change to Guardians becomes official
Rare original copy of the U.S. Constitution sold to Ken Griffin, head of hedge fund Citadel, for $43.2 million - world record price for a book, historical document or manuscript
US jury clears Kyle Rittenhouse (18) of murder for fatally shooting two people and injuring a third during racial justice protests in Kenosha, Wisconsin
General elections in Malaysia produce the country's first ever hung parliament with opposition leader Anwar Ibrahim securing the most seats
Gunman opens fire at LGBTQ nightclub Club Q in Colorado Springs, killing five people and injuring 19 in an apparent hate crime
Brazil records its highest-ever temperature - 44.8C (112.6F) in Araçuaí, Minas Gerais state
Far-right politician Javier Milei elected President of Argentina

ICC Men's Cricket ODI World Cup, Narendra Modi Stadium, Ahmedabad, India: Australia beats India by 6 wickets (with 42 balls remaining) for 6th ODI triumph; Player of the Match: Travis Head (AUS) 137 (120)
Ukraine for the first time fires American-made longer-range missiles into Russia, after the US eases restrictions on their use
Christopher Columbusfirst reaches Puerto Rico, inhabited by the Taíno on his 2nd voyage to the Caribbean
Battle at Milan: EmperorCharles V's pontifical, Spanish and German troops beat French forces and occupy Milan
Florentine Giulio de' Medici chosen as PopeClement VII- rules until 1534
Lewis and ClarkExpedition, led byMeriwether LewisandWilliam Clark, reaches the Pacific Ocean, the first European Americans to cross the West
Franz Schubert's Symphony No. 4, "The Tragic", premieres in Leipzig, more than 20 years after the composer's death
Alfred Tennysonbecomes British Poet Laureate, succeeding William Wordsworth
William MagearTweed"Boss Tweed", of Tammany Hall (NYC) convicted of defrauding city of $6M, sentenced to 12 years' imprisonment
Former Dutch premierAbraham Kuyperdenies corruption
NY receives firstMarconiwireless transmission from Italy
Samuel Goldfish, Edgar and Archibald Selwyn establish Hollywood's Goldwyn Pictures; Goldfish later changes his name to Goldwyn
Hungarian composer Béla Bartòk's orchestral work "Dance Suite" premieres, celebrating 50th anniversary of cities of Buda and Pest uniting
Leon Trotskyis expelled from the Politburo in the Soviet Union
Shaft & Thyssen demandAdolf Hitlerbecome German chancellor
Joseph Goebbelsvisits "German Theatre in the Netherlands"
World War II: U.S. PresidentFranklin Rooseveltannounces the 6th War Loan Drive, aimed at selling $14 billion USD in war bonds to help pay for the war effort.
PrinceRainier IIIcoronation as 30th ruling Prince of Monaco
US GeneralEisenhowerbecomes supreme commander of NATO-Europe
Roy Campanellanamed NL MVP on his 30th birthday
US VPRichard Nixonvisits Hanoi
Houston Oilers quarterbackGeorge Blandapasses for 7 touchdowns in a 49-13 rout of NY Titans at Jeppesen Stadium, Houston
Fidel Castroaccepts removal of Soviet weapons
Willy Brandt's SPD wins West German election

Film adaptation of "One Flew Over the Cuckoo's Nest" based stage adaptation of novel by Ken Kesey, directed by Miloš Forman and starringJack NicholsonandLouise Fletcher, is released (Academy Awards Best Picture 1976)
Reds 2nd basemanJoe Morganis named NL MVP
American publishing heiressPatty Hearstis freed on $15 million bail, pending appeal of bank robbery conviction
George Harrisonreleases single "This Song", an expression of his frustration at being sued for copyright infringement
Egyptian PresidentAnwar Sadatarrives in Israel
Astros signNolan Ryan, to record 4 year, $4.5 million contract
Chuck Berryreleased from prison for income tax evasion conviction
CBS TV bansCalvin Kleinjean ad featuringBrooke Shields
Edmonton Oilers beat NJ Devils, 13-4,Wayne Gretzkycalls the Devils "a Mickey Mouse organization"
Jari Kurribecomes the first (and only) Finnish player to score five goals in a game
NY MetDwight Gooden, 20, is youngest to be named NL Rookie of Year
US PresidentRonald Reaganand Soviet leaderMikhail Gorbachevmeet for the first time
Phillies 3rd basemanMike Schmidtwins NL MVP
Pittsburgh Pirates outfielderBarry Bondswins NL MVP
Baltimore Orioles shortstopCal Ripkenwins his 2nd AL MVP
German tennis No. 1Steffi Grafoutlasts No. 2Anke Huber6–1, 2–6, 6–1, 4–6, 6–3 to win the WTA Tour Championship at Madison Square Garden, NYC
Home crowd favoriteBoris Beckerwins his 3rd and final ATP Tour World Championship tennis title with a 7–6, 6–0, 7–6 win over AmericanMichael Changin Frankfurt, Germany
"Sex and Longing" starringSigourney Weavercloses at Cort Theatre in NYC
Vincent van Gogh's "Portrait of the Artist Without Beard" sells at auction for $71.5 million
Swiss tennis starMartina Hingiswins WTA Tour Championship at Madison Square Garden, NYC; beatsMonica Seles, 6–7, 6–4, 6–4
Nonesuch Records releases "Travelogue", Canadian singer-songwriterJoni Mitchell's 18th studio album, a collection of re-worked versions her own songs, backed by an orchestra
Innovative video game console the Nintendo Wii is released in North America by Nintendo PresidentSatoru Iwata
Roger Federercaptures his 3rd Tennis Masters Cup title with a comfortable 6–0, 6–3, 6–4 win over AmericanJames Blakein the final in Shanghai, China
"Harry Potterand the Deathly Hallows – Part 1" the 7th film based on the books byJ. K. Rowlingis released worldwide
David Blaineperforms in the TV special "David Blaine: Real or Magic" on ABC
American singer-songwriter and piano manBilly Joelreceives Library of Congress Gershwin Prize at the White House in Washington, D.C.
Martin Truex Jr., driving for Furniture Row Racing, wins Ford EcoBoost 400 at Homestead-Miami Speedway, Florida to claim Monster Energy NASCAR Cup Series by 5 points fromKyle Busch
Ivanka Trumpsent "hundreds" of emails from her personal account about government business, according to Washington Post report

Rams beat Kansas City Chiefs, 54-51 at Los Angeles Memorial Coliseum; 3rd-highest scoring game in NFL history; first time 2 teams score 50 points in same game;Patrick Mahomes' 6 TD passes for KC most in "Monday Night Football" history
LeBron Jameshits 25 points, 11 rebounds & 10 assists as LA Lakers' beat Oklahoma City Thunder, 112-107 to become first player in NBA history to record a triple-double against all 30 franchises
The Weekndreleases his single "Blinding Lights" - goes on to become the longest charting song on Billboards Top 100 at 90 weeks
Adelereleases her fourth studio album "30"
Rounder Records releases "Raise The Roof", the 2nd collaborative album by American bluegrass singerAlison Kraussand British rock vocalistRobert Plant
Novak Đokovićof Serbia wins a record seventh ATP World Tour Finals beating Italian Jannik Sinner 6-3, 6-3 in Turin, Italy
Tsarevich Ivan Ivanovich dies after being attacked by his fatherIvan the Terriblethree days earlier
The Mayflower reaches Cape Cod and explores the coast
US PresidentAbraham Lincolndelivers his Gettysburg address beginning; "Four score and seven years ago..."
US Senate rejects the Treaty of Versailles by a vote of 55 to 39; Congress two years later approves resolution ending hostilities with Germany and Austria-Hungary
Zimbabwe PresidentRobert Mugaberefuses to resign in a TV speech after being ousted as leader of ruling ZANU-PF party
